license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: Remote Desktop Services: Session logon succeeded
entries.summary: RDP session logon succeeded on the target host.
entries.details: Generated on the target host when a user successfully logs on via RDP. Key fields: User (DOMAIN\username), SessionID, and Address (source IP of the connecting client). This is one of the most reliable indicators of a completed RDP interactive logon and should be correlated with Security 4624 LogonType 10 using the same username and timestamp. The Address field is critical for attribution and is present even when NLA is used.

investigationPivots: Pivot on User and Address to trace who connected from where
Correlate SessionID with EID 23/24/25 to track full session lifecycle
Correlate with Security EID 4624 LogonType 10 using username and timestamp
commonFalsePositives: Jump server or bastion host connections

entries.title: Remote Desktop Services: Shell start notification received
entries.details: Generated immediately after EID 21 when the user's shell (explorer.exe or equivalent) starts within the RDP session. Confirms that the logon progressed to a full interactive desktop, not just an authentication handshake. Key fields: User and SessionID. Absence of this event after EID 21 may indicate session was terminated before the desktop initialized.

investigationPivots: Pair with EID 21 using SessionID to confirm full desktop initialization
Missing EID 22 after EID 21 may indicate a failed or aborted session

entries.title: Remote Desktop Services: Session logoff succeeded
entries.details: Generated when a user fully logs off from an RDP session (session is destroyed). Key fields: User and SessionID. Distinguish from EID 24 (disconnect): a logoff terminates the session and releases all resources, whereas a disconnect leaves the session running on the server. Correlate with EID 21 using SessionID to measure full session duration.

investigationPivots: Correlate SessionID with EID 21 to calculate total session duration
Absence of EID 23 with EID 24 present means the session is still running disconnected

entries.title: Remote Desktop Services: Session has been disconnected
entries.summary: RDP session disconnected but not logged off.
entries.details: Generated when the RDP client disconnects without logging off — the session remains running on the server and can be reconnected. Key fields: User, SessionID, and Address (source IP of the disconnecting client). A session in the disconnected state is still accessible via the console or a reconnecting RDP client. Monitor for long-lived disconnected sessions as they may persist attacker access.

investigationPivots: Correlate SessionID with EID 25 to detect reconnection to this disconnected session
Long-lived disconnected sessions with no subsequent EID 23 may indicate persistent attacker access
Address field captures the client IP at time of disconnect
commonFalsePositives: Network interruption causing unintended RDP disconnect
Users who close the RDP window without formally logging off

entries.title: Remote Desktop Services: Session reconnection succeeded
entries.summary: RDP client reconnected to an existing disconnected session.
entries.details: Generated when an RDP client successfully reconnects to a previously disconnected session (a session that was preserved after EID 24). Key fields: User, SessionID, and Address (source IP of the reconnecting client). Unlike EID 21 (new logon), EID 25 indicates resumption of an existing session without a fresh authentication. Monitor for reconnections from a different source IP than the original session to detect session hijacking or unauthorized access to abandoned sessions.

investigationPivots: Reconnections from a different IP than the original session (EID 24 Address) may indicate session hijacking
Correlate SessionID with EID 24 to determine how long the session was disconnected before reconnection
commonFalsePositives: Legitimate user reconnecting after a network drop
Admin resuming an existing session from a different workstation
